title: zanonia indica subsp. orientalis
intro: Zanonia indica subsp. orientalis, also known as the Asian bitter apple or wild cucumber, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cucurbitaceae. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In traditional medicine, Zanonia indica subsp. orientalis is often prepared as a decoction, infused tea, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and treat various ailments.
